======================================================================
FORWARD SIGNALING ANALYSIS — METHODOLOGY
  Node of interest : MYB46
  Network          : filtered_GT_normalized.csv
  Hop radius       : 10
  Attractor solver : BoNesis — complete Boolean network attractor landscape.
                     Finds ALL attractors reachable from each initial condition.

  Subgraph: all nodes reachable from MYB46 in ≤10 directed steps.
  Boolean rules: any activator is sufficient (OR); any suppressor dominates (AND-NOT).
  Rule form: (act1 | act2 | ...) & !sup1 & !sup2 & ...
  Update scheme: synchronous (all nodes updated simultaneously each step).

  Experimental conditions compared:
    (A) Dark background      — all nodes initialised to 0 (all pathways inactive)
    (B) Permissive background — all nodes initialised to 1 (all pathways active)
  Within each background, resting (MYB46=0) vs. perturbed (MYB46=1) are compared.
  A node is classified as:
    Activated  : stable OFF in resting AND stable ON  in ALL perturbed attractors
    Suppressed : stable ON  in resting AND stable OFF in ALL perturbed attractors
    Robust     : activated/suppressed consistently in BOTH backgrounds

  Sink nodes: regulated by others but regulating nothing in the subgraph.
  Evaluated post-hoc by applying their Boolean rule to settled attractor states.
  Pre-filtered: only sinks that change state between conditions are reported.
  ! = experimental perturbation node (mutant/process/phenotype) — not a gene product
